concept.title: Local z/OS projects
concept.shortdesc: Local z/OS projects are defined on the local workstation and are not associated with a remote system.

conbody.p: When you first define your local project, you set values for properties such as compiler options. You can also assign a property value at the level of a folder or file, file, as described later.
You can export the values of project properties to an XML file. You and other team members can quickly define other local projects by importing the values from that file.

The workstation-based compiler performs syntax checking in local projects. As is true in MVS projects, any copybooks, include files, or macros are checked if they are referenced in the source and are accessible. Errors are displayed in the Remote Error List view, and you can double-click an error entry to open the file (potentially an included file) where the error was found.
